justification: All 5 ACs implemented with correct HTTP codes and error codes (200, 409/EMAIL_ALREADY_IN_USE, 400/INVALID_EMAIL_FORMAT, 404/USER_NOT_FOUND, 200 idempotent). AC5 same-email idempotency is explicitly distinct from AC2 conflict check — same email short-circuits before conflict detection. No external validation library used (simple regex, with a note about the trade-off). 13 tests cover all 5 ACs with explicit names. acceptance_criteria_coverage maps all 5 ACs to named tests.

Guard ordering is deliberate and documented: format → exist → idempotent → conflict → write, minimizing unnecessary DB calls

## Implementation Summary

**What was built:** Express.js `PATCH /users/:id/email` handler with full test suite.

**Approach:** Factory function `makePatchUserEmail(userRepository)` returns an async Express handler. Guards execute in priority order — format validation first (cheap, no I/O), then existence check, then same-email short-circuit, then conflict check, then write. This ordering means no unnecessary DB calls when earlier guards fire
